narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school and then hearing no must feel like a door slamming shut just as you're reaching for the handle. Our prediction model looked at your profile and estimated a 49% chance you'd struggle to pass the bar exam, which is why we had to make this tough call on your application. What stood out most was your LSAT score of 29, the biggest signal pulling toward that risk—it just didn't shine as brightly as we typically see in students who power through to bar success. Layered on top were your Year 1 and Year 3 deciles, both at 1.0, showing early and ongoing challenges in keeping pace with your peers at school, which the model saw as the next strongest hurdles. Your middle family income bracket added another layer, as it often means fewer resources for the intense prep that bar passage demands. Even with your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.4, your Asian background, full-time study plans, and being female, all nudged the picture toward higher risk in the model's view—these factors together painted a story of someone who might face an uphill battle. It's not that you're not capable or driven; it's that this specific combination has tripped up others before, and we want to set you up for paths where you'll thrive. Imagine retaking the LSAT to boost that score, or finding programs that build stronger academic momentum from the start—those could rewrite your story. You've got a target_law passion score of 1.0, which tells me your heart is in this; don't let one prediction dim that fire. We're rooting for you to find the right door that opens wide.
